project.name: 01_music_recommendation
project.description: A visual scene-aware music recommendation system that uses AI (CLIP) to analyze uploaded photos and recommend matching music based on scene attributes. It features a Flask API backend with a dual-layer recommendation engine, and a React 19 frontend with ECharts visualizations and GSAP animations.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview for the visual scene-aware music recommendation system, covering architecture, API endpoints, directory structure, supported scenes, and quick-start instructions.

nodes.name: scene_attributes.json
nodes.summary: Defines the scene attribute schema with 9 attribute dimensions (energy, tempo, valence, acousticness, etc.), scene prototypes with attribute scores, music target mapping rules, and genre prior rules for the recommendation engine.

nodes.name: scene_profiles.json
nodes.summary: Defines attribute profiles for 5 scenes (cafe, gym, study_room, night_street, mountain) with per-dimension energy, tempo, valence, acousticness, and instrumentalness scores used for music recommendation matching.

nodes.name: readme.md
nodes.summary: Documentation for the Spotify sample dataset describing data source, data dictionary with field meanings (track_id, danceability, energy, genre, etc.), and usage notes for the 32,833-song CSV file.

nodes.name: spotify_songs.csv
nodes.summary: Large dataset of 32,833 Spotify tracks with audio features (danceability, energy, key, loudness, mode, speechiness, acousticness, instrumentalness, liveness, valence, tempo), genre labels, and popularity scores used as the music recommendation corpus.

nodes.name: download_manifest.json
nodes.summary: Download manifest listing 5 real test images (cafe, gym, mountain, night street, study room) with source URLs and metadata for validating visual scene recognition and CLIP-based classification.

nodes.name: ai_context_summary.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive AI conversation summary documenting project goals, completed modules, data status, recommendation pipeline details, visual module state, and prioritized next-step recommendations.

nodes.name: cf_datasets_evaluation.md
nodes.summary: Evaluation of open-source collaborative filtering datasets (ListenBrainz, Million Song Dataset, Last.fm, Melon Playlist, Spotify MPD) for hybrid recommendation integration, with landing recommendations.

nodes.name: current_architecture_diagram.md
nodes.summary: ASCII architecture diagram documenting the two-path recommendation system with 7 layers from input through scene understanding, attribute mapping, recommendation engine, music data, and service/presentation.

nodes.name: current_progress.md
nodes.summary: Detailed project progress report covering completed features (recommendation pipeline, visual CLIP recognition, evaluation tools), key architectural reflections, and prioritized P0-P6 next-step planning.

nodes.name: detailed_proposal.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive implementation proposal covering the full recommendation system architecture with scene attribute layer design, two-stage cascaded recall, multi-criteria ranking strategy, data pipeline, visual module integration plans, and cross-modal retrieval evolution.

nodes.name: next_step_plan.md
nodes.summary: Next-step optimization plan with task breakdown across 6 directions (recommendation quality, visual upgrade, confidence distribution, collaborative filtering, dataset expansion, presentation) for a 3-member team.

nodes.name: next_step_plan_5members.md
nodes.summary: Extended next-step optimization plan for a 5-member team with individual member responsibilities, collaboration patterns, weekly cadence, and execution roadmap for the recommendation system project.

nodes.name: proposal.md
nodes.summary: Brief project proposal summary outlining the vision for visual scene-aware context music recommendation, including system architecture, data sources, and core innovations.

nodes.name: recommend_system.md
nodes.summary: Technical reference on recommendation system techniques used in the project, covering content-based filtering, knowledge-based rules, multi-criteria scoring, cascaded recall, zero-shot CLIP classification, and phased collaborative filtering integration plans.

nodes.name: structrue.mmd
nodes.summary: Mermaid flowchart diagram illustrating the full music recommendation system architecture with 8 layers including input, scene understanding, attribute mapping, recommendation orchestration, shared engine, music data, service, and evaluation components.

nodes.name: structure.md
nodes.summary: Detailed project architecture documentation covering the dual-path recommendation architecture (legacy and attribute-based), core module responsibilities, data flows, scene attribute layer with 9 dimensions, and technology stack.

nodes.name: structure_picture.md
nodes.summary: Concise 6-layer system architecture description designed for AI diagram generation, covering input, scene understanding, recommendation engine, music feature, service, and presentation layers with module dependencies.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Vite + React + TypeScript frontend documentation covering setup instructions, React Compiler configuration, and ESLint flat config expansion for the recommendation system dashboard.

nodes.name: components.json
nodes.summary: Shadcn/ui component configuration defining base-nova style, Tailwind CSS settings, Lucide icon library, and path aliases (components, utils, ui, hooks) for the React frontend.

nodes.name: eslint.config.js
nodes.summary: ESLint flat configuration for the React TypeScript frontend, importing recommended TypeScript, React Hooks, and React Refresh lint rules with dist/ global ignores.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry point for the React frontend application with Chinese-language meta description, dark theme-color, root div mount point, and main.tsx module script reference.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Frontend project metadata defining Vite + React build scripts, runtime dependencies (React, ECharts, GSAP), and dev tooling (TypeScript, ESLint).

nodes.name: App.css
nodes.summary: Legacy CSS styling for a default Vite+React starter template; not actively used by the current scene-recommendation UI.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Main application component containing the scene-aware music recommendation cockpit with scene switching, image upload recognition, attribute radar charts, genre distribution, and full recommendation queue. Also hosts the product-design and technology info pages.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-file architecture with 40+ local components and utility functions; uses GSAP for scroll-triggered animations, ECharts for radar/bar charts, and motion for fade-in effects.

nodes.name: animated-shiny-text.tsx
nodes.summary: Animated shiny text component that renders a CSS gradient shine effect across text content.

nodes.name: badge.tsx
nodes.summary: Badge UI component with variant-based styling (default, secondary, destructive, outline, ghost, link) built on @base-ui/react primitives.

nodes.name: blur-fade.tsx
nodes.summary: Scroll-triggered blur-and-translate fade-in animation component using motion (framer-motion) with configurable direction, offset, delay, and blur amount.

nodes.name: border-beam.tsx
nodes.summary: Animated rotating border beam decoration using CSS offset-path and motion animations with configurable colors, size, and direction.

nodes.name: button.tsx
nodes.summary: Button UI component with variant and size presets (default, outline, secondary, ghost, destructive, link; xs through icon-lg) built on @base-ui/react button primitive.

nodes.name: card.tsx
nodes.summary: Card component family (Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardAction, CardContent, CardFooter) providing a composable container system with size variants.

nodes.name: grid-pattern.tsx
nodes.summary: SVG grid pattern overlay component with configurable grid size, dash array, and optional highlighted squares.

nodes.name: number-ticker.tsx
nodes.summary: Animated number ticker that counts up or down on scroll using motion spring physics, with configurable start value, direction, delay, and decimal places.

nodes.name: separator.tsx
nodes.summary: Visual separator/divider component with horizontal and vertical orientation support, built on @base-ui/react separator primitive.

nodes.name: tabs.tsx
nodes.summary: Tabs component family (Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent) with default and line variants and vertical orientation support, built on @base-ui/react tabs primitive.

nodes.name: index.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet defining a dark green-themed design system with CSS custom properties, Tailwind CSS v4 integration, Google Fonts (Manrope, Space Grotesk), and base layer resets.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses OKLCH color space for consistent perceptual luminance across the dark theme palette.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: API client providing three functions to interact with the backend: fetch scene list, fetch scene-based recommendations, and infer scene from uploaded image via CLIP classifier.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: TypeScript type definitions for scene keys, API response schemas (scene summaries, recommendations, image recognition results), and data structures used across the frontend.

nodes.name: utils.ts
nodes.summary: Utility function `cn` that merges Tailwind CSS class names using clsx and tailwind-merge for conflict resolution.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: React application entry point that mounts the App component into the DOM root element with StrictMode.

nodes.name: tsconfig.app.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for the application source code, targeting ES2023 with bundler module resolution, React JSX transform, and strict linting rules.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: Root TypeScript configuration file that delegates to tsconfig.app.json and tsconfig.node.json via project references.

nodes.name: tsconfig.node.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration for Node.js tooling files (vite.config.ts), targeting ES2023 with bundler module resolution.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite bundler configuration with React and Tailwind CSS v4 plugins, @ path alias resolution, and development server proxy for the Python Flask backend.

nodes.name: hexToRgba
nodes.summary: Converts a hex color string to rgba() format with a specified alpha channel, handling both 3-digit and 6-digit hex values.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root component that routes between the recommendation app, product-design page, and technology page based on URL pathname.

nodes.name: RecommendationApp
nodes.summary: Main recommendation cockpit component with scene sidebar, hero image display, upload-to-match with drag-and-drop, attribute radar chart, genre distribution chart, track queue, and real-time animation orchestration via GSAP.

nodes.name: ProductDesignPage
nodes.summary: Product design documentation page presenting the product vision, user requirements, scope definition (MVP vs future), success metrics, and design boundaries.

nodes.name: TechnologyPage
nodes.summary: Technology documentation page explaining the recommendation pipeline with architecture layers, processing flow cards, scoring signals, attribute-to-music mapping, and technical dependencies.

nodes.name: ProductPillarCard
nodes.summary: Displays a product pillar (target user, core problem, product proposition) with icon and description in a card layout.

nodes.name: RequirementStageCard
nodes.summary: Renders a numbered requirement stage with step title and bullet points for the product design view.

nodes.name: ScopeCard
nodes.summary: Displays a product scope column (MVP must-have, next phase, out of scope) with an accent color bar and items list.

nodes.name: ProductRequirementTabs
nodes.summary: Tabbed view toggling between structured requirement stages and a full product design diagram image.

nodes.name: ArchitectureTabs
nodes.summary: Tabbed view toggling between interactive layered architecture display and a full software architecture diagram image.

nodes.summary: Renders the five-layer recommendation architecture as horizontally connected layer groups, plus dependency cards for song data, scene config, and vision model.

nodes.name: ArchitectureLayerGroup
nodes.summary: Renders a single architecture layer with accent bar, title, subtitle, and node cards, plus an arrow connector to the next layer.

nodes.summary: Renders a single node card within an architecture layer, showing icon, title, and description.

nodes.summary: Displays a dependency card for the architecture view, showing an external resource with icon, label, title, and description.

nodes.name: TechScoreCard
nodes.summary: Displays a scoring signal explanation card with label, value badge, and description.

nodes.name: MiniMetric
nodes.summary: Compact metric display with label, animated number ticker value, and suffix, used in the sidebar info panel.

nodes.name: SummaryMetric
nodes.summary: Summary metric bar with label, animated number ticker, and suffix for the match summary section.

nodes.name: MetricBar
nodes.summary: Horizontal bar chart component with label, percentage value, animated gradient bar, and optional hint text, supporting green and gold color tones.

nodes.name: TrackQueueItem
nodes.summary: Compact track card showing rank, title, artist, genre tag, and final score, used for the top 5 recommendation preview queue.

nodes.name: TrackListRow
nodes.summary: Full track list row showing rank, title, artist, genre, feature similarity and energy badges, and final score in a grid layout.

nodes.name: AlertCard
nodes.summary: Warning alert card with an amber accent that displays an error or warning message with an icon.

nodes.name: LoadingGrid
nodes.summary: Loading state placeholder grid with four pulsing skeleton cards matching the insights section layout.

nodes.name: LoadingCard
nodes.summary: Single pulsing skeleton card used within the loading grid placeholder.

nodes.name: buildRadarOption
nodes.summary: Builds an ECharts radar chart option object from attribute scores and scene accent colors, displaying the scene's personality fingerprint across 9 dimensions.

nodes.name: buildGenreOption
nodes.summary: Builds an ECharts horizontal bar chart option from genre priors data, showing the top genre preferences for the current scene.

nodes.name: mergeImageResponse
nodes.summary: Merges an image recognition response into a scene recommendation response, overwriting scene data with image-inferred attribute scores, music targets, and recommendations.

nodes.summary: Returns a human-readable Chinese label for the recognition model, distinguishing CLIP AI, fallback mock mode, and unknown models.

nodes.summary: Animated entrance component that applies configurable blur and translate effects controlled by scroll-intersection via motion's useInView hook.

nodes.name: BorderBeam
nodes.summary: Animated border beam decoration using CSS offset-path for rotation around a rounded container, with configurable colors, size, speed, and direction.

nodes.name: Button

nodes.name: Card
nodes.summary: Card container component with configurable size (default/sm) and consistent border, padding, and ring styling.

nodes.name: CardHeader
nodes.summary: Card header section with adaptive grid layout for titles, descriptions, and action buttons.

nodes.name: CardTitle
nodes.summary: Card title element with heading font and medium weight, responsive to card size variant.

nodes.name: CardDescription
nodes.summary: Card description text with muted foreground color for supplementary content.

nodes.name: CardAction
nodes.summary: Card action button container positioned in the top-right corner of the card header grid.

nodes.name: CardContent
nodes.summary: Card content section with responsive padding that adjusts based on card size variant.

nodes.name: CardFooter

nodes.summary: SVG-based grid pattern overlay with configurable dimensions, dash array, and optional highlighted square coordinates.

nodes.name: NumberTicker
nodes.summary: Animated number counter that springs from start to target value on scroll into view, with configurable direction, delay, and decimal precision.

nodes.name: Separator
nodes.summary: Visual divider line component supporting horizontal and vertical orientations via @base-ui/react separator primitives.

nodes.name: Tabs
nodes.summary: Tabs root container with orientation support (horizontal/vertical) built on @base-ui/react tabs primitive.

nodes.name: TabsList
nodes.summary: Tabs list container with default and line variants, handling tab layout and active state styling.

nodes.name: TabsTrigger
nodes.summary: Individual tab trigger button with active state indicators, focus styles, and variant-aware underlines.

nodes.name: TabsContent
nodes.summary: Tab content panel container rendered when its associated trigger is active.

nodes.name: readJson
nodes.summary: Generic typed JSON fetch helper that checks response status, handles error objects, and throws descriptive error messages.

nodes.name: fetchScenes
nodes.summary: Fetches the list of available scene summaries from the backend /scenes endpoint.

nodes.name: fetchSceneRecommendation
nodes.summary: POSTs a scene key to the backend to retrieve an attribute-based music recommendation response.

nodes.name: inferSceneFromImage
nodes.summary: Uploads an image file to the backend as multipart form data for CLIP-based scene inference with attribute-based recommendation fallback.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Merges Tailwind CSS class names using clsx for conditional classes and tailwind-merge for conflict resolution.

nodes.name: frame-manifest.json
nodes.summary: JSON manifest listing captured demo frames with metadata including source URL, creation timestamp, durations, and file paths for GIF assembly.

nodes.name: clip_stability_test.csv
nodes.summary: Tabular results of CLIP-based scene classification stability tests across sample images, with per-image fields for expected scene, confidence, and match status.

nodes.name: clip_stability_test.json
nodes.summary: JSON report with summary metrics and per-image CLIP classification stability test results, including accuracy rates and status counts.

nodes.name: mock_stability_test.csv
nodes.summary: Tabular results of mock classifier stability tests for baseline comparison against CLIP-based results, with per-image match and confidence data.

nodes.name: mock_stability_test.json
nodes.summary: JSON report with summary metrics and per-image mock classifier stability test results, used as a baseline to compare against CLIP-based classification.

nodes.name: scene_overlap_comparison.csv
nodes.summary: CSV comparing song recommendation overlap between old genre-based and new attribute-based recommendation methods across all scene pairs.

nodes.name: scene_overlap_comparison.json
nodes.summary: Comprehensive JSON evaluation report comparing old and new recommendation methods with overlap matrices, per-scene quality metrics, feature distance matrices, and same-scene drift analysis.

nodes.name: requirements.txt
nodes.summary: Project dependency manifest listing Python packages for the music recommendation system including torch, pandas, scikit-learn, flask, and open-clip-torch.

nodes.name: batch_clip_stability_test.py
nodes.summary: In-process batch test script for CLIP scene classification stability that loads the model once, iterates over test images, and writes per-image JSON and CSV reports with accuracy metrics.
nodes.languageNotes: Python script using argparse for CLI, sys.path injection to import from src/, and incremental report saving after each image.

nodes.name: build_demo_gif.py
nodes.summary: Builds an animated demo GIF from captured frame images by reading a JSON frame manifest, resizing and converting frames to palette mode, and assembling with per-frame durations.

nodes.name: build_share_bundle.py
nodes.summary: Generates a static shareable demo bundle by computing recommendations for all scene prototypes, copying assets and diagrams, and writing a self-contained JavaScript data file.
nodes.languageNotes: Python script that imports from src/ modules (config, attribute_mapper, recommender) and uses sys.path injection for intra-project module resolution.

nodes.name: capture_demo_frames.mjs
nodes.summary: Playwright-based automated demo frame capture script that launches the web app, navigates scenes, uploads test images, and saves screenshot frames with a JSON manifest.

nodes.name: download_sample_images.py
nodes.summary: Downloads scene-relevant sample images from Wikimedia Commons using the MediaWiki API, with HTML cleaning, license extraction, filename sanitization, and deduplication by page ID.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses requests.Session for connection reuse, Wikimedia Commons API for image search, and comprehensive license metadata extraction from Commons imageinfo.

nodes.name: launch_dynamic_web.py
nodes.summary: Launches the dynamic Flask web application with stdout/stderr redirection to log files and PID tracking for process management.

nodes.name: manage_dynamic_web.py
nodes.summary: Process lifecycle manager for the Flask web app supporting start, stop, and status commands with PID file tracking, port listener detection, health checks, and Windows scheduled task integration.
nodes.languageNotes: Windows-aware process management using tasklist for PID checks, netstat for port listening detection, and schtasks for scheduled task creation.

nodes.name: run_dynamic_web.cmd
nodes.summary: Windows batch script that directly launches the dynamic web application via Python without process management.

nodes.name: scene_overlap_evaluation.py
nodes.summary: Evaluates and compares old genre-based and new attribute-based recommendation methods by computing overlap matrices, per-scene quality metrics, feature distance matrices, and same-scene drift analysis.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses pandas and numpy for matrix computations, with Jaccard overlap, genre entropy, feature distance (L2 norm), and same-scene drift metrics.

nodes.name: start_dynamic_web.cmd
nodes.summary: Windows batch script that starts the dynamic web application via the manage script with errorlevel propagation.

nodes.name: start_dynamic_web.ps1
nodes.summary: PowerShell script that starts the dynamic web application by invoking the Python manage script with the start command.

nodes.name: stop_dynamic_web.cmd
nodes.summary: Windows batch script that stops the dynamic web application via the manage script with errorlevel propagation.

nodes.name: stop_dynamic_web.ps1
nodes.summary: PowerShell script that stops the dynamic web application by invoking the Python manage script with the stop command.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Parses command-line arguments for the CLIP stability test including image root, classifier type, top-k, output directory, and optional CLIP checkpoint path.

nodes.name: find_images
nodes.summary: Recursively finds all image files with supported extensions under a given root directory and returns them sorted.

nodes.name: build_summary
nodes.summary: Computes aggregate accuracy metrics and status counts from per-image classification results.

nodes.name: write_json
nodes.summary: Writes a dictionary payload as formatted JSON to a file path with UTF-8 encoding.

nodes.name: write_csv
nodes.summary: Writes per-image classification results as a CSV file with predefined field columns using DictWriter.

nodes.name: save_reports
nodes.summary: Creates output directory and saves both JSON and CSV report files with naming based on classifier type.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full CLIP stability test pipeline: argument parsing, image discovery, classifier loading, per-image classification with timing, incremental report saving, and summary output.

nodes.name: fit_frame
nodes.summary: Resizes an image to a fixed 960x540 canvas preserving aspect ratio with center positioning.

nodes.name: build_gif
nodes.summary: Reads the frame manifest JSON, loads and processes each frame image, and assembles them into an animated GIF with per-frame durations.

nodes.name: _ensure_share_dirs
nodes.summary: Creates the share data, assets, and diagram directories if they do not already exist.

nodes.name: _clear_directory
nodes.summary: Empties a directory by removing all files and subdirectories, recreating it if it does not exist.

nodes.name: _normalize_value
nodes.summary: Converts numpy scalars to native Python types and rounds floats for JSON-safe serialization.

nodes.name: _serialize_recommendations
nodes.summary: Converts a pandas DataFrame of recommendations into a JSON-serializable list of dicts with normalized values.

nodes.name: _build_scene_payload
nodes.summary: Builds a complete scene data payload with recommendations, attribute scores, prototype info, and hero image for the share bundle.

nodes.name: _copy_scene_image
nodes.summary: Copies a scene's hero image from the source directory to the share assets directory and returns the relative path.

nodes.name: _copy_diagram
nodes.summary: Copies a diagram file to the share diagrams directory and returns its relative asset path.

nodes.name: _build_samples
nodes.summary: Builds a list of sample scene entries with labels, scene IDs, and hero image paths for the share bundle sample display.

nodes.name: build_share_bundle
nodes.summary: Orchestrates share bundle generation: loads processed song data, builds scene payloads for all prototypes, copies assets and diagrams, and writes the self-contained JavaScript data file.

nodes.name: captureFrame
nodes.summary: Captures a page screenshot, saves it as a PNG file, records frame metadata with duration, and pushes to the frames array.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full demo frame capture pipeline: launches Playwright browser, navigates through app scenes with scroll interactions and image upload, captures frames, and writes the JSON manifest.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Parses command-line arguments for image download including scene selection, output directory, image count, thumbnail width, and rate limiting.

nodes.name: build_session
nodes.summary: Creates a requests Session with a common User-Agent header for Wikimedia Commons API calls.

nodes.name: sanitize_filename
nodes.summary: Sanitizes a filename by removing non-alphanumeric characters and replacing whitespace with underscores.

nodes.name: strip_title_prefix
nodes.summary: Removes common Wikimedia file title prefixes like 'File:' from the beginning of a title string.

nodes.name: strip_known_suffix
nodes.summary: Strips known suffix patterns like language codes from filenames to produce cleaner output names.

nodes.name: clean_html_text
nodes.summary: Strips HTML tags, unescapes HTML entities, and normalizes whitespace in a text string.

nodes.name: choose_image_url
nodes.summary: Selects the best image URL from Wikimedia imageinfo, preferring thumbnails over full-resolution URLs.

nodes.name: extract_license
nodes.summary: Extracts comprehensive license metadata from Wikimedia Commons imageinfo including license name, URL, artist, credit, description, and usage terms.

nodes.name: search_commons
nodes.summary: Queries the Wikimedia Commons API for images matching a search query with configurable result limit and thumbnail width.

nodes.name: download_file
nodes.summary: Downloads a file from a URL and writes it to a local path using streaming chunked transfer.

nodes.name: make_manifest_record
nodes.summary: Creates a structured manifest record with scene name, image metadata, license info, and local file path.

nodes.name: download_scene_images
nodes.summary: Downloads images for a single scene from Wikimedia Commons with deduplication by page ID, filename sanitization, and rate-limited requests.

nodes.name: save_manifest
nodes.summary: Saves the download manifest JSON file to the output directory with formatted indentation.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full image download pipeline: parses args, creates HTTP session, iterates over scenes to download images, and saves the consolidated manifest.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Launches the Flask web app with stdout/stderr log redirection, writes PID file, and executes the app module via runpy.

nodes.name: run_command
nodes.summary: Runs a shell command via subprocess with optional check mode and returns the completed process result.

nodes.name: read_pid
nodes.summary: Reads the process PID from the PID file, returning the integer PID or None if the file is missing or invalid.

nodes.name: is_pid_running
nodes.summary: Checks if a given PID is currently running by querying the Windows task list with error filtering.

nodes.name: find_listener_pid
nodes.summary: Finds the PID of a process listening on a given port using the Windows netstat command.

nodes.name: is_healthy
nodes.summary: Performs an HTTP health check against the web app at localhost to verify it is responding.

nodes.name: cleanup_stale_state
nodes.summary: Removes the PID file if the recorded process is no longer running.

nodes.name: resolve_running_pid
nodes.summary: Resolves the current running PID by checking the PID file and port listener, writing a corrected PID file if needed.

nodes.name: clear_logs
nodes.summary: Deletes the stdout and stderr log files to clear previous session logs.

nodes.name: ensure_task
nodes.summary: Creates or updates a Windows scheduled task that runs the launch script as the current user with highest privileges.

nodes.name: run_task
nodes.summary: Triggers the Windows scheduled task to start the web app immediately.

nodes.name: wait_until_started
nodes.summary: Polls the process status and health check endpoint until the app is confirmed running or the timeout expires.

nodes.name: start
nodes.summary: Starts the web application by cleaning stale state, ensuring the scheduled task exists, running it, and waiting for the health check to pass.

nodes.name: stop
nodes.summary: Stops the web application by killing the running process, deleting the PID file, and stopping the scheduled task.

nodes.name: status
nodes.summary: Reports the current status of the web application including PID, health check result, and running state.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that dispatches to start, stop, or status subcommands based on command-line arguments.

nodes.name: compute_overlap_matrix
nodes.summary: Computes the Jaccard overlap matrix between all scene pairs based on their top-k recommended song IDs.

nodes.name: summarize_per_scene_quality
nodes.summary: Computes per-scene quality metrics including genre entropy, artist diversity, popularity, and final score averages for the top-k recommendations.

nodes.name: compute_feature_distance_matrix
nodes.summary: Computes the pairwise L2 feature distance matrix between scenes based on their mean feature vectors.

nodes.name: compute_same_scene_drift
nodes.summary: Measures the change in song composition and feature centroid for each scene between old and new recommendation methods.

nodes.name: run_comparison
nodes.summary: Full comparison pipeline: loads data, runs old and new recommendation methods, computes all evaluation matrices, prints detailed reports, and saves JSON and CSV output files.

nodes.name: visualize_recommendations.py
nodes.summary: Generates comparison visualizations and dashboards showing overlap, feature analysis, genre distribution, and similarity scores between old scene-based and new attribute-based recommendation engines.

nodes.name: build_overlap_matrix
nodes.summary: Computes a matrix of Jaccard-like overlap scores between recommendation results across all scene pairs.

nodes.name: plot_overlap_heatmap
nodes.summary: Plots a side-by-side heatmap comparing recommendation overlap between old and new engines for quick visual assessment.

nodes.name: plot_feature_comparison
nodes.summary: Plots per-scene bar charts comparing mean audio feature values (energy, acousticness, etc.) between old and new recommendation results.

nodes.name: plot_genre_distribution
nodes.summary: Plots pie charts showing genre distribution of top-K recommendations per scene for both recommendation engines.

nodes.name: plot_similarity_scores
nodes.summary: Plots box plots comparing feature similarity score distributions across scenes between old and new engines.

nodes.name: plot_dashboard
nodes.summary: Generates a comprehensive multi-panel dashboard combining overlap heatmap, feature comparison bar chart, and stacked genre distribution chart.

nodes.name: print_stats
nodes.summary: Prints detailed statistical comparison between old and new recommendation results including genre diversity, feature means, and overlap rates.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that orchestrates data loading, collecting results from both engines, generating all visualizations, and printing stats.

nodes.name: share/README.md
nodes.summary: Documents how to generate the static share bundle and explains the offline preview workflow for sharing recommendation results without a running backend.

nodes.name: share-data.js
nodes.summary: Pre-generated static recommendation data bundle containing scene results, attribute profiles, genre priors, music targets, and song recommendations for all five scenes in the offline share page.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Self-contained static share page displaying pre-generated scene recommendations with attribute profiles, genre distribution charts, product design diagrams, and technical architecture visualizations.

nodes.name: share/intro.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive project introduction and presentation guide covering the scene-to-music recommendation system architecture, core technical highlights, key algorithms, and suggested presentation flow.

nodes.name: api.py
nodes.summary: Flask API server providing REST endpoints for scene listing, scene-based recommendation, attribute-based recommendation, image recommendation via file upload, and static frontend serving.

nodes.name: df_to_response
nodes.summary: Converts a recommendation DataFrame into a JSON-serializable response dictionary with scene profile metadata.

nodes.name: image_recommend_to_response
nodes.summary: Structures image-based recommendation results into a rich JSON response including attribute scores, prototype matches, and fallback information.

nodes.name: attribute_recommend_to_response
nodes.summary: Structures attribute-based recommendation results into a detailed JSON response with source scene, attribute summary, genre priors, and music targets.

nodes.name: get_scenes
nodes.summary: Flask GET endpoint returning all available scenes with descriptions, preferred genres, target features, and attribute scores.

nodes.name: recommend_by_scene
nodes.summary: Flask POST endpoint that accepts a scene name and top-K parameter, runs the legacy scene-based recommendation, and returns results as JSON.

nodes.name: recommend_by_scene_attributes
nodes.summary: Flask POST endpoint that accepts scene name or custom attribute scores and runs the attribute-based recommendation pipeline.

nodes.name: recommend_by_image_endpoint
nodes.summary: Flask POST endpoint accepting image file upload with classifier preference and attribute options, saving to temp file and calling the vision recommendation pipeline.

nodes.name: index
nodes.summary: Serves the frontend React dist build if available, falling back to the Flask-rendered HTML template.

nodes.name: attribute_mapper.py
nodes.summary: Maps scene attribute scores to music target features, tolerances, genre priors, and ranking weights through configurable JSON-based mapping rules forming the core attribute-to-music translation layer.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a JSON config-driven architecture where attribute-to-music mappings are defined declaratively in scene_attributes.json. The _weighted_sum helper computes weighted combinations of attribute scores using config-defined coefficients.

nodes.name: normalize_attribute_scores
nodes.summary: Normalizes attribute scores to a config-defined range (default 0-10) using min-max scaling from the JSON config.

nodes.name: _tempo_to_norm
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw tempo value to 0-1 range using the min and max tempo from the song dataset.

nodes.name: map_attributes_to_music_targets
nodes.summary: Translates normalized attribute scores into six music target features (energy, danceability, acousticness, instrumentalness, valence, tempo) using weighted rules from config.

nodes.name: map_attributes_to_tolerances
nodes.summary: Derives per-feature tolerance ranges from attribute scores, defining how strictly feature values must match the target.

nodes.name: map_attributes_to_genre_priors
nodes.summary: Translates attribute scores into genre preference weights using config-defined genre prior rules, enabling genre-aware recommendation.

nodes.name: select_preferred_genres
nodes.summary: Selects the top-N preferred genres from genre priors that exceed a minimum score threshold.

nodes.name: get_attribute_ranking_weights
nodes.summary: Computes scene-specific ranking weights for feature similarity, genre match, and popularity based on normalized attribute scores.

nodes.name: build_target_array
nodes.summary: Converts music target features into a numpy array with tempo_norm replacing raw tempo for vector operations.

nodes.name: match_scene_prototypes
nodes.summary: Compares attribute scores against predefined scene prototypes using cosine similarity and returns the top-K closest matching scenes.

nodes.name: summarize_attribute_scores
nodes.summary: Generates a human-readable summary of the top-N attributes with their scores, labels, descriptions, and emoji icons from the config.

nodes.name: get_feature_importance
nodes.summary: Computes the relative importance of each music feature for the given attribute scores by analyzing mapping rule contributions.

nodes.name: build_attribute_profile
nodes.summary: Assembles a complete attribute profile for a scene by running attribute scores through all mapping stages: music targets, genre priors, tolerances, ranking weights, prototype matching, and feature importance.

nodes.name: config.py
nodes.summary: Central configuration constants defining project directory paths, feature keys, numeric features, standard column names, and default hyperparameters for the recommendation pipeline.

nodes.name: data_loader.py
nodes.summary: Loads the sample Spotify CSV dataset from disk into a pandas DataFrame using the configured data path.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Command-line entry point for the recommendation system supporting scene-based, all-scenes, and image-based recommendation modes with argparse configuration and detailed output.

nodes.name: print_summary
nodes.summary: Prints a dataset overview including total songs, unique genres, and genre-aggregated statistics for popularity, energy, and valence.

nodes.name: print_recommendations
nodes.summary: Formats and prints recommendation results as a styled table with rank, title, artist, genre, and feature scores.

nodes.name: mode_scene
nodes.summary: Runs single-scene recommendation with detailed console output showing attribute scores, music targets, genre priors, and top-K results.

nodes.name: mode_image
nodes.summary: Runs image-based recommendation from the command line, displaying recognized scene, confidence, model info, and top-K song results.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point that parses CLI arguments, loads data, preprocesses, and dispatches to all-scenes, single-scene, or image recommendation mode with error handling.

nodes.name: preprocess.py
nodes.summary: Preprocesses raw Spotify song data by cleaning genre columns, normalizing numeric features, filling missing values, removing duplicates, and sorting by popularity.

nodes.name: preprocess_songs
nodes.summary: Cleans and normalizes raw song data: standardizes genres/subgenres/titles/artists, converts numeric features, fills missing values with median, deduplicates by song_id, and sorts by popularity.

nodes.name: ranker.py
nodes.summary: Implements multi-criteria ranking for song recommendations, combining weighted cosine feature similarity, genre match score, popularity boost, and artist/genre diversity penalty.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a greedy iterative selection algorithm that applies diversity penalties at each step to ensure variety in recommended results.

nodes.name: compute_cosine_similarity
nodes.summary: Computes weighted cosine similarity between each song vector and the target feature vector, supporting per-feature importance weights.

nodes.name: compute_genre_prior_score
nodes.summary: Scores songs based on how well their genres match the preferred genre priors, with higher weights for higher-priority genres.

nodes.name: compute_genre_match_score
nodes.summary: Computes a combined genre matching score using both genre priors and binary preferred-genre/subgenre matches.

nodes.name: compute_diversity_penalty
nodes.summary: Applies penalty scores to already-selected songs when the same artist or genre appears multiple times in the recommendation list.

nodes.name: generate_recommendation_reason
nodes.summary: Generates human-readable Chinese-language recommendation explanations describing why songs fit the scene, combining attribute descriptions, genre hints, and feature matches.
nodes.languageNotes: Generates Chinese text explanations using attribute profile data, joining description parts with Chinese punctuation.

nodes.name: rank
nodes.summary: Main ranking function that computes combined scores from feature similarity, genre match, and popularity, then applies iterative greedy selection with diversity penalties to produce the final top-K recommendations.

nodes.name: recall.py
nodes.summary: Implements the recall/retrieval stage, narrowing candidate songs through genre filtering, feature tolerance matching, and soft scoring to produce a manageable pool for ranking.

nodes.name: recall_by_genre
nodes.summary: Filters songs by preferred genres and subgenres, expanding to include all songs when minimum candidate threshold is not met.

nodes.name: recall_by_features
nodes.summary: Filters candidates by feature value ranges, first applying strict tolerance bounds then relaxing when minimum count is not reached.

nodes.name: recall_by_feature_scoring
nodes.summary: Soft-scoring recall that ranks candidates by exponential feature distance and selects the top-N as an alternative to strict range filtering.

nodes.name: select_recall_pool
nodes.summary: Selects a random sample of candidates of the desired pool size with optional seeding for reproducibility.

nodes.name: recall
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full recall pipeline: genre filter, feature scoring or strict filter, and final pool selection.

nodes.name: recommender.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the end-to-end recommendation pipeline including scene-based recommendation, attribute-based recommendation via build_attribute_profile, and image-based recommendation with classifier selection.

nodes.name: recommend
nodes.summary: Legacy scene-based recommendation: loads scene profile, gets genres, target features, tolerances, and ranking weights, then calls recall and rank.

nodes.name: recommend_by_attributes
nodes.summary: Attribute-driven recommendation using the full attribute profile: builds target vectors, feature importance weights, then recalls and ranks candidates.

nodes.name: recommend_by_attribute_scores
nodes.summary: High-level attribute recommendation: builds an attribute profile from scene/attribute scores, calls recommend_by_attributes, and generates recommendation reasons.

nodes.name: recommend_all_scenes_by_attributes
nodes.summary: Runs attribute-based recommendation across all available scene prototypes and returns a dictionary of scene-to-results mappings.

nodes.name: _get_classifier
nodes.summary: Selects and instantiates the appropriate scene classifier (mock or CLIP) based on the prefer parameter, with diagnostic logging.

nodes.name: recommend_by_image
nodes.summary: End-to-end image recommendation: classifies the image scene, builds attribute profile (or uses legacy scene profile), generates recommendation reasons, and returns full results with diagnostics.

nodes.name: scene_mapper.py
nodes.summary: Loads and queries scene profile configurations from JSON, providing functions to list scenes and retrieve scene-specific target features, preferred genres, tolerances, and ranking weights.

nodes.name: get_target_features
nodes.summary: Retrieves target music features for a scene, normalizing tempo to 0-1 range using the dataset min and max.

nodes.name: get_tolerances
nodes.summary: Retrieves per-feature tolerance ranges for a scene, normalizing tempo tolerances to the 0-1 range.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Flask-rendered HTML template providing the fallback frontend interface when the React dist build is unavailable, offering basic scene selection and recommendation display.

nodes.name: test.py
nodes.summary: Placeholder test file containing only a print statement, no actual test logic or assertions.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package init file that re-exports get_scene_classifier and BaseSceneClassifier from the scene_classifier module.

nodes.name: scene_classifier.py
nodes.summary: Image scene classification module with a base abstract classifier, a color-statistics-based mock classifier, and a CLIP-based classifier with lazy loading, multi-prompt scoring, and softmax confidence.
nodes.languageNotes: The CLIPSceneClassifier uses lazy model loading (loaded on first classify call) and generates multi-prompt text embeddings for each scene using OpenCLIP. Mock classifier uses simple HSV color statistics as a fallback.

nodes.name: MockSceneClassifier
nodes.summary: Fallback scene classifier using hand-tuned HSV color profiles to classify images into scenes (cafe, gym, study_room, night_street, mountain) without any ML dependencies.

nodes.name: CLIPSceneClassifier
nodes.summary: Primary scene classifier using OpenCLIP with multi-prompt text embeddings per scene, lazy model loading, and softmax-normalized confidence scores.

nodes.name: get_scene_classifier
nodes.summary: Factory function that returns the best available classifier (CLIP preferred, Mock fallback) with diagnostic logging and on-demand validation.

nodes.name: test_vision.py
nodes.summary: Integration test script for the vision-based recommendation pipeline, running mock-classifier image recommendations on five test scenes and writing results to a text file.

layers.name: API Layer
layers.description: Flask REST API endpoints for scene listing, scene-based and attribute-based recommendation, image upload recognition, and static frontend serving

layers.name: Service Layer
layers.description: Core recommendation engine pipeline including cascaded recall, multi-criteria ranking, attribute mapping, scene profile mapping, data preprocessing, and CLIP-based visual scene classification

layers.name: UI Layer
layers.description: React 19 frontend application with scene-aware recommendation cockpit, ECharts attribute radar charts and genre distributions, GSAP animations, and shadcn/ui components

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Spotify track dataset with 32,833 songs and audio features used as the music recommendation corpus

layers.name: Configuration Layer
layers.description: Scene attribute and profile definitions, build system configuration (TypeScript, Vite, ESLint, package dependencies), download manifests, and Python central config constants

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Project overview, architecture diagrams and proposals, progress tracking, team planning documents, recommendation system technical reference, and frontend/share README guides

layers.name: Test and Evaluation Layer
layers.description: CLIP stability tests, scene overlap evaluation, demo frame capture, comparison visualizations, test reports in CSV and JSON format, and integration test scripts

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Web application launch and process management scripts, demo GIF builder, share bundle builder, image downloader, and Windows batch/PowerShell management commands

layers.name: Share Layer
layers.description: Self-contained static shareable presentation bundle with pre-generated scene recommendations, architecture diagrams, and offline preview page

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with README.md to understand the project's purpose: a visual scene-aware music recommendation system that uses AI to analyze uploaded photos and recommend matching music. The README maps the overall architecture (Frontend to REST API to Recommendation Engine), lists the five supported scenes, and provides quick-start instructions for running the app.

tour.title: System Architecture Blueprint
tour.description: The Mermaid diagram and architecture documents give a visual map of the entire system across 8 layers: from image input and scene understanding through the recommendation engine, music data, and service/presentation layers. This blueprint is your reference for understanding how data flows from a user's photo upload to a curated playlist recommendation.

tour.title: Backend REST API
tour.description: The Flask API server is the bridge between the frontend and the recommendation engine. It exposes five endpoints: listing supported scenes, recommending by scene name, recommending by custom scene attributes, recommending by uploaded image, and serving the static frontend. Each endpoint delegates to the recommendation pipeline and formats results as JSON responses for the React UI.
tour.languageLesson: Python Flask routes use decorators like @app.route('/api/scenes') to bind URL patterns to handler functions. The jsonify helper converts Python dicts into JSON responses with the correct Content-Type header.

tour.title: Recommendation Engine Orchestrator
tour.description: The recommender module orchestrates the end-to-end recommendation pipeline. It provides three modes: scene-based recommendation (using the five predefined scenes), attribute-based recommendation (from custom scene attribute scores), and image-based recommendation (using the CLIP or Mock visual classifier). It ties together the recall, ranking, and attribute mapping stages.
tour.languageLesson: Python's Facade pattern is used here: the recommender module exposes a clean public API while hiding the complexity of cascaded recall, multi-criteria ranking, and attribute mapping behind simple function calls like recommend(scene_id).

tour.title: Cascaded Recall Layer
tour.description: The recall module implements the first stage of recommendation: narrowing down 33,000 songs to a manageable candidate pool. It uses genre filtering as the primary sieve, followed by feature tolerance matching and soft scoring. The cascaded design progressively tightens constraints, ensuring the ranker receives only relevant candidates rather than the entire dataset.
tour.languageLesson: Cascaded recall is a common information retrieval pattern. Each stage applies a different filter: Stage 1 uses pandas DataFrame boolean indexing for fast genre filtering, Stage 2 applies per-feature tolerance ranges, and Stage 3 scores candidates rather than hard-filtering them.

tour.title: Multi-Criteria Ranking
tour.description: The ranker module scores each candidate song on multiple dimensions: cosine similarity between song features and target features, genre match score using prior probabilities, popularity boost for well-known tracks, and a diversity penalty that discourages too many songs from the same artist or genre. The final score is a weighted combination that produces a balanced, relevant playlist.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-criteria ranking uses weighted linear combination: final_score = w1 * similarity + w2 * genre_score + w3 * popularity - w4 * diversity. The diversity penalty uses set-based deduplication to prevent the top-10 from being dominated by a single artist.

tour.title: Scene-to-Music Attribute Mapping
tour.description: The attribute mapper translates abstract scene attribute scores (like 'energy: 0.8', 'valence: 0.3') into concrete music parameters: target audio features, tolerance windows, genre priors, and ranking weights. This is the core intelligence of the recommendation engine -- it encodes the musical characteristics of each scene type. The scene_mapper loads predefined profiles for the five canonical scenes.
tour.languageLesson: Configuration-driven mapping separates business logic from configuration data. The mapper reads JSON rules that define how attribute scores map to music features, allowing new scene types to be added without changing code -- only the JSON configs need updating.

tour.title: Scene Configuration Profiles
tour.description: These two JSON files define the entire scene knowledge base. scene_attributes.json specifies the 9-dimensional attribute schema and the mapping rules from attributes to music targets. scene_profiles.json defines the attribute scores for each of the five scenes (cafe, gym, study_room, night_street, mountain). Together they form the domain model that makes the recommendation engine scene-aware.
tour.languageLesson: JSON config files act as a declarative domain-specific language. Each scene profile stores 9 attribute values (0-1 scale) that describe the musical character of a space: high-energy/low-acousticness for a gym, low-energy/high-acousticness for a study room.

tour.title: Visual Scene Classification
tour.description: The vision module provides two classifiers for recognizing scenes from images. The MockSceneClassifier uses simple color statistics (brightness, saturation, color variance) for lightweight classification without ML dependencies. The CLIPSceneClassifier uses OpenAI's CLIP model with multi-prompt scoring and softmax confidence for production-grade scene understanding. A factory function selects the appropriate classifier at runtime.
tour.languageLesson: Python's Strategy Pattern via abstract base class (ABC): BaseSceneClassifier defines the interface, and MockSceneClassifier and CLIPSceneClassifier provide different implementations. The factory function get_scene_classifier() returns the right strategy based on configuration.

tour.title: Music Dataset and Preprocessing
tour.description: The recommendation corpus is a 33,000-song Spotify dataset with audio features like danceability, energy, valence, tempo, acousticness, and genre labels. The preprocessing pipeline cleans genre columns, normalizes numeric features to 0-1 scale, fills missing values, removes duplicates, and sorts by popularity. The data_loader module provides a single function to load this dataset into a pandas DataFrame.
tour.languageLesson: Data preprocessing is critical for recommendation quality. Numerical feature normalization ensures that features with different scales (tempo 0-200 vs. energy 0-1) contribute equally to similarity computations. Genre cleaning standardizes multi-label genre strings into a consistent format.

tour.title: Frontend Application Entry
tour.description: The React 19 frontend is a Vite + TypeScript application. main.tsx mounts the App component into the DOM root. App.tsx houses the main recommendation cockpit with scene switching, image upload and CLIP-based recognition, ECharts radar charts for attribute visualization, genre distribution charts, and a full recommendation queue. It also hosts product-design and technology-info pages.
tour.languageLesson: React 19's use() hook enables reading from Promises directly in render. The frontend uses GSAP for scroll-triggered animations and ECharts for interactive radar and bar charts. Tailwind CSS v4 with CSS custom properties handles the dark-green theme.

tour.title: Frontend API Client and Type System
tour.description: The frontend communicates with the Flask backend through a typed API client in api.ts. It provides three functions: fetchScenes (listing scenes), fetchSceneRecommendation (getting recommendations), and inferSceneFromImage (uploading a photo for CLIP classification). The types.ts file defines TypeScript interfaces for scene keys, API response schemas, and data structures used across the UI, ensuring type safety between the frontend and backend.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript discriminated unions model API responses: the image recognition result type uses a 'method' field ('clip' vs. 'mock') as a discriminant, enabling type-narrowing switches in the component code.

tour.title: Share Bundle and Deployment
tour.description: The share bundle is a self-contained static HTML page with pre-computed recommendations for all five scenes, making it possible to share results without a running backend. The share-data.js file contains serialized scene results, attribute profiles, and song recommendations. The build_share_bundle.py script generates this bundle by running the recommendation pipeline for each scene and outputting a portable JavaScript data file.
tour.languageLesson: Static site generation pre-computes dynamic content at build time. The Python build script runs the full recommendation pipeline, serializes results into a JSON-like JavaScript object, and produces a single HTML file that works offline -- no Flask server required.
